project: PRIME IQE — Axiom Federal Solutions / Walker Contractors LLC
description: All T.E.S.T. v2 evaluation cases across 6 categories. Run via GitHub Actions at 04:00 CT daily and after every agent execution.

tier_system.TIER_1_LOG: Single failure — write to test_results, re-test next cycle
tier_system.TIER_2_ALERT: 3+ consecutive failures — add to BRANDI morning brief as WARNING
tier_system.TIER_3_HALT: Critical failure — disable affected agent, BRANDI sends URGENT email

categories.label: Data Integrity
categories.description: Validates that SCOUT writes clean, complete opportunity records. No nulls on critical fields, valid dates, correct NAICS format.

categories.label: Score Validation
categories.description: Validates JUDGE scoring output. All scores in 0-100 range. No null scores after JUDGE ran. 30-day mean within calibration band. LEASE scores separately validated.

categories.label: Financial Math
categories.description: Validates EXEC financial calculations are physically possible. Negative interest = bug. Retainage > contract value = bug. These are HALT-tier — impossible math stops the agent immediately.

categories.label: Auth & API Keys
categories.description: Confirms all API keys are present and valid. SAM.gov 401/403 halts SCOUT immediately. Missing env vars halt on first occurrence.

categories.label: API Contracts
categories.description: Validates that external APIs still return the schema we coded against. Schema drift = ALERT (not HALT — SAM can change mid-day). api_schemas table stores expected field lists.

categories.label: Email Delivery
categories.description: Confirms BRANDI sent the morning brief within the last 25 hours. Missing email = ALERT after 3 days. One missed email does not halt.

categories.label: Database Health
categories.description: Confirms all 30 tables are accessible. Any inaccessible table = immediate HALT — database failures are system-wide. Also checks audit_log activity and supplier DB population.

tests.name: scout_solicitation_not_null
tests.description: Checks that all opportunities written in the last 24 hours have a non-null solicitation_number. A null solicitation_number means SCOUT wrote a partial record — downstream dedup will break.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE solicitation_number IS NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
tests.remediation: Check scout.js upsertOpportunity() — ensure solicitation_number is always mapped from SAM response before insert. Verify SAM API is returning the field.

tests.name: scout_contract_value_positive
tests.description: No opportunity can have a negative contract value. A negative value means the value parser hit a string or formatting issue. Zero is allowed (value not yet announced).
tests.query: opportunities WHERE contract_value < 0 AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
tests.remediation: Check scout.js value parsing — ensure parseFloat() with fallback to 0, not negative. Check for values stored with parentheses like (500,000).

tests.name: scout_deadline_valid
tests.description: Response deadlines should not already be expired by 7+ days when written. An already-expired deadline means SCOUT is writing stale data from a previous cycle without dedup.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND response_deadline IS NOT NULL AND response_deadline < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
tests.remediation: Check NAICS dedup Map in scout.js — stale opportunities may be re-inserted. Verify SAM.gov query uses active=true filter.

tests.name: scout_naics_six_digits
tests.description: All NAICS codes must be exactly 6 digits (e.g., '238350'). Non-6-digit codes mean the SAM API returned a partial code or SCOUT truncated it. JUDGE scoring breaks on invalid NAICS.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND naics IS NOT NULL
tests.remediation: Check scout.js naics field mapping — String(o.naicsCode) trim and validate length before insert. May need padding for 5-digit legacy codes.

tests.name: judge_scores_in_range
tests.description: All PRIME Scores and ACQ Scores written in the last 24 hours must be between 0 and 100 inclusive. Scores outside this range mean a weight misconfiguration in judge.js.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE (prime_score < 0 OR prime_score > 100 OR acq_score < 0 OR acq_score > 100) AND scored_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
tests.remediation: Check judge.js scoreConstruction() and scoreSupply() final Math.min(100, Math.max(0, total)) clamps. Verify no factor weight array sums exceed 100 points.

tests.name: judge_no_null_after_scoring
tests.description: If an opportunity has a scored_at timestamp but null prime_score AND null acq_score, JUDGE crashed mid-write. Both being null after scoring is scored is a bug.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE scored_at IS NOT NULL AND prime_score IS NULL AND acq_score IS NULL AND scored_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
tests.remediation: Check judge.js error handling in scoreAndUpdate() — confirm try/catch doesn't swallow score write. Verify updatePayload includes at least one score field.

tests.name: judge_score_calibration
tests.description: 30-day PRIME Score mean should be between 25 and 85. Below 25 = JUDGE is too harsh (scoring everything as junk). Above 85 = JUDGE is too generous (scoring everything as strong bid). Only runs if 10+ scores exist.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE prime_score IS NOT NULL AND scored_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
tests.remediation: Review LEDGER monthly calibration report. If mean < 25, reduce penalty weights. If mean > 85, reduce bonus weights. Run ledger-monthly.js to recalibrate.

tests.name: judge_lease_scores_in_range
tests.description: LEASE Scores for real estate opportunities must be between 0-100. Separate from PRIME Score validation because LEASE uses a different 5-factor model.
tests.query: opportunities WHERE lease_score IS NOT NULL AND (lease_score < 0 OR lease_score > 100) AND scored_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
tests.remediation: Check judge.js scoreRealEstate() final clamp. Verify ownership_match_bonus (30pts) + other factors don't exceed 100 without clamping.

tests.name: exec_interest_positive
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. Prompt payment interest must always be positive. Negative interest would mean EXEC calculated money flowing the wrong direction — a critical math bug that could produce a wrong demand letter.
tests.query: prompt_payment_claims WHERE interest_amount < 0 AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
tests.remediation: Check exec-prompt-payment.js interest calculation: (principal × PPA_RATE × days_late / 365). Verify days_late > 0 before calculating. EXEC is disabled until fixed.

tests.name: exec_days_late_reasonable
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. Days late > 365 means a data integrity bug — government doesn't owe interest for more than a year on a single invoice. Over 365 likely means date parsing failed.
tests.query: prompt_payment_claims WHERE days_late > 365 AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
tests.remediation: Check exec-prompt-payment.js date diff: Math.floor((paymentDate - invoiceDate) / 86400000). Verify both dates are valid before diff. Check for epoch=0 fallback dates.

tests.name: exec_retainage_valid
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. Retainage held cannot exceed contract value — that would mean we're holding more money than the contract is worth, which is physically impossible.
tests.query: active_contracts WHERE retainage_held > contract_value AND retainage_held IS NOT NULL AND contract_value IS NOT NULL
tests.remediation: Check exec-retainage.js calculation: retainage_held = contract_value × (retainage_rate / 100). Ensure rate is stored as percentage (5-10), not decimal (0.05-0.10).

tests.name: exec_retainage_rate_valid
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. Retainage rate must be between 0-100%. A rate below 0 or above 100 is impossible and indicates the rate was stored as a decimal and then used as a percentage (0.10 → 10%).
tests.query: retainage_tracker WHERE retainage_rate < 0 OR retainage_rate > 100
tests.remediation: Confirm retainage_rate is stored as percentage points (5, 10) not decimal (0.05, 0.10). Check insert in exec-retainage.js — standardize to percentage format.

tests.name: vault_bond_amount_valid
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. Bid bond amounts must be positive and cannot exceed 10× the contract value. A bond larger than 10× the contract is a calculation error — bonds are typically 10-20% of contract value.
tests.query: bid_bonds WHERE (bond_amount <= 0 OR bond_amount > contract_value * 10) AND bond_amount IS NOT NULL AND contract_value IS NOT NULL
tests.remediation: Check vault.js bond calculation: bond_amount = contract_value × bond_rate. Verify bond_rate is decimal (0.10) not percentage (10). Standard construction bonds are 5-15% of contract value.

tests.name: scout_sam_key_present
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER (first occurrence). SAM_API_KEY environment variable must be set. Without it, SCOUT cannot call SAM.gov and will write zero opportunities. Checked on every T.E.S.T. run.
tests.query: process.env.SAM_API_KEY
tests.expected: SAM_API_KEY environment variable set and non-empty
tests.remediation: Add SAM_API_KEY to GitHub Actions repository secrets. Get key from api.sam.gov → Account → API Keys. Free tier allows 450 calls/day.

tests.name: api_sam_gov_auth
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. SAM.gov API must return 200 (not 401/403). A 401 means the key is invalid or expired. A 403 means the account was suspended. Either halts SCOUT immediately.
tests.query: GET https://api.sam.gov/opportunities/v2/search?limit=1&api_key={SAM_API_KEY}
tests.remediation: Log in to api.sam.gov and verify API key status. Keys expire annually — renew before expiry. If suspended, contact SAM.gov help desk. SCOUT is disabled until resolved.

tests.name: api_sam_gov_schema
tests.description: SAM.gov response must include expected fields: opportunityId, title, solicitationNumber, fullParentPathName, naicsCode, type, responseDeadLine, award. If any disappear, scout.js breaks silently.
tests.query: api_schemas WHERE api_name = 'sam_gov' AND status = 'valid'
tests.expected: All expected schema fields present in SAM.gov response
tests.remediation: SAM.gov changed their API schema. Update scout.js field mapping to use new field names. Update api_schemas table with current expected_schema. ALERT fires after 3 consecutive mismatches.

tests.name: brandi_email_sent
tests.description: BRANDI must send the morning brief daily at 6:00 AM CT to PrimeOpps1@gmail.com. T.E.S.T. checks the audit_log for a 'Morning brief sent' entry within the last 25 hours (gives 1-hour buffer).
tests.query: audit_log WHERE agent = 'BRANDI' AND action = 'Morning brief sent' AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '25 hours'
tests.expected: At least 1 morning brief sent in last 25 hours
tests.remediation: Check GitHub Actions brandi-daily.yml run history. If workflow ran but no email: verify SENDGRID_API_KEY in secrets, check SendGrid activity for bounces. If workflow didn't run: check cron syntax 'cron: 0 11 * * *' (11 UTC = 6 AM CT).

tests.name: db_all_tables_accessible
tests.description: HALT TRIGGER. All 30 core tables must be accessible via Supabase anon key. Any inaccessible table = immediate system-wide HALT. Checks: opportunities, bids, active_contracts, compliance, incumbents, competitor_prices, job_costs, audit_log, co_contacts, prompt_payment_claims, retainage_tracker, sub_payments, debrief_tracker, compliance_matrices, capability_statements, certified_payroll, sub_plans, cpars_ratings, contract_modifications, sam_health_checks, distributor_prices, gao_protests, osdbu_events, bid_bonds, prime_help, test_results, api_schemas, system_config, suppliers, supplier_matches.
tests.query: SELECT id FROM {each_table} LIMIT 1
tests.remediation: Check Supabase dashboard → Table Editor for missing tables. Run sql/schema-gap-fix.sql and sql/create-all-tables.sql to recreate any missing tables. Check RLS policies — if enabled, anon key may be blocked.

tests.name: db_audit_log_active
tests.description: audit_log must have entries from the last 30 days. No entries = agents haven't run in a month, which means GitHub Actions workflows are disabled or crashing silently.
tests.query: audit_log WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
tests.expected: At least 1 audit entry in last 30 days
tests.remediation: Open GitHub Actions on axiom-federal-solutions/prime-iqe-afs and check if workflows are enabled. Look for failed runs. Verify SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY secrets are set.

tests.name: supplier_db_populated
tests.description: suppliers table should have at least 1 record after the first RECON scan. Empty suppliers table = RECON has never run successfully or the SAM supplier API returned nothing.
tests.query: suppliers LIMIT 1
tests.expected: At least 1 supplier record in database
tests.remediation: Run RECON workflow manually from GitHub Actions. If it runs but suppliers stays empty, check recon.js supplier fetch — verify USASpending API is returning awardees for your NAICS codes.

meta.schedule: Daily 04:00 CT + triggered after SCOUT, JUDGE, EXEC, BRANDI
